description:
You are a security auditor who has been asked to get a quick overview of the
    filesystem permissions used for every regular file inside the directory
    /home/user/audit_dir (including anything below it).  
    Management wants a **frequency table** that tells, for each distinct
    permission string (the first ten characters shown by “ls -l”), how many
    regular files use that exact string.  
    The company’s automated compliance system will later ingest your result, so
    the format of the report must be **exactly** as described below.

    1. Inspect every regular file found in   
       • /home/user/audit_dir itself  
       • any of its sub-directories (there is presently one called “logs”).  

       Ignore directories, symbolic links, devices, sockets, etc.—only count
       entries whose `ls -l` line starts with “-”.

    2. Produce a frequency table that has **two columns separated by one
       single space** in this order:  
       • COUNT (the number of occurrences, written as a plain decimal
         integer, no leading zeroes)  
       • PERMISSION-STRING (exactly the first ten characters from “ls -l”)  

       The table must be sorted
       • primarily by COUNT in descending numeric order  
       • secondarily (when counts are equal) by PERMISSION-STRING in ordinary
         ASCII lexicographic order.  

    3. Save the table to the file
       /home/user/permission_frequency.log  

       The file must contain nothing else—no headings, no blank lines, no
       trailing spaces.  Each line ends with a single newline (`\n`).

    4. Verify the contents visually in the terminal so you are sure it is
       correct.

    Hint: one classic pipeline for this job involves a combination of
    “ls -lR”, “grep/awk/cut”, “sort”, and “uniq -c”, followed by a final
    “sort -nr”.

    The automated checker will succeed only if the file
    /home/user/permission_frequency.log exists and its contents are **exactly**
    correct.
truth:
Pre-created directory structure and permissions (already available when the
    session starts):

    /home/user/audit_dir/                    drwxr-xr-x  (directory)
    ├── config.cfg           -rw-r--r-- 1 user user ...
    ├── run.sh               -rwxr-xr-x 1 user user ...
    ├── temp.log             -rw-rw-rw- 1 user user ...
    ├── public.txt           -rw-rw-rw- 1 user user ...
    └── logs/                drwxr-xr-x  (directory)
        └── error.log        -rw-r--r-- 1 user user ...

    Therefore the correct frequency table that **must** be written to
    /home/user/permission_frequency.log is:

    2 -rw-r--r--
    2 -rw-rw-rw-
    1 -rwxr-xr-x
name: task_000000_5eac88ac
